attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Common.m_gameView?.SlotReels.SetReelSymbolMode( col, mode );\\n    }\\n    //#endregion 方法\\n\\n    //#region 測試功能\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 註冊測試按鍵\\n     */\\n    private RegisterTestKeyCodes() {\\n <observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Added FeatureWheel trail animation event definition</title>
  <subtitle>New FLY_EVENT.PLAY_FEATURE_WHEEL_TRAIL_ANIMATION event declared with roundIndex-based left/right trail selection</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>FLY_EVENT.PLAY_FEATURE_WHEEL_TRAIL_ANIMATION event constant added with value "FLY_PLAY_FEATURE_WHEEL_TRAIL_ANIMATION"</fact>
    <fact>Event accepts roundIndex (0-based): 0-2 use left trail, 3-5 use right trail</fact>
    <fact>Event parameters include startWorldPosition, endWorldPosition, skinName (default/Multiplier/Shift/Upgrade), and optional callback</fact>
    <fact>Trail animation uses Spine positioned at start with Trail_End_Control bone targeting end position</fact>
    <fact>Event documentation added at line 58-68 in FlyManager.ts</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Added a new event definition to FLY_EVENT enum for feature wheel trail animations. The event PLAY_FEATURE_WHEEL_TRAIL_ANIMATION will coordinate trail visual effects during feature wheel gameplay, using a dual-prefab approach split by round index (left trails for rounds 0-2, right trails for rounds 3-5). The event signature documents five parameters: roundIndex for trail selection logic, world positions for start and end points, skinName for visual variation (with fallback to "default" for invalid values), and an optional completion callback. This establishes the interface contract that calling code will use to trigger trail animations.</narrative>

    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>

  </files_read>

    <file>assets/Script/FlyManager.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
The user is editing FlyManager.ts to add two new Prefab properties for the FeatureWheel trail animations - one for the left trail (rounds 0-2) and one for the right trail (rounds 3-5). These are inspector-exposed properties decorated with @property that will be configured in the Cocos Creator editor.

This is adding the necessary class properties to support the dual prefab approach discussed in the design phase.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Added left and right trail prefab properties to FlyManager</title>
  <subtitle>Two Prefab properties added for FeatureWheel trail animations split by round index</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>m_featureTrailPrefabL property added for rounds 0-2 (left trail, EyeStrike2_Function_Trail_L)</fact>
    <fact>m_featureTrailPrefabR property added for rounds 3-5 (right trail, EyeStrike2_Function_Trail_R)</fact>
    <fact>Both properties decorated with @property for Cocos Creator inspector exposure</fact>
    <fact>Properties added at line 113-124 in FlyManager.ts</fact>
    <fact>Tooltips clearly document the round mapping: 123輪/0~2 for left, 456輪/3~5 for right</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Added two Prefab member properties to the FlyManager component for the feature wheel trail animation system. The m_featureTrailPrefabL and m_featureTrailPrefabR properties represent the left and right trail animation prefabs respectively, with the split based on round index (0-2 uses left, 3-5 uses right). These properties are decorated with @property to expose them in the Cocos Creator inspector, allowing designers to assign the appropriate Spine animation prefabs. The tooltip documentation explicitly maps the rounds (123/456) to their 0-based indices (0-2 / 3-5) and identifies the expected prefab names (EyeStrike2_Function_Trail_L/R). This dual-prefab approach enables different trail directions based on game round progression.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/FlyManager.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

Common.m_gameView?.SlotReels.SetReelSymbolMode( col, mode );\\n    }\\n    //#endregion 方法\\n\\n    //#region 測試功能\\n    /**\\n     * @ch 註冊測試按鍵\\n     */\\n    private RegisterTestKeyCodes() {\\n content.thinking:
The user is implementing the FeatureWheel trail animation feature. This edit adds several constants related to the feature trail system:

1. FEATURE_TRAIL_POOL_KEY_L and FEATURE_TRAIL_POOL_KEY_R - pool keys for left and right trails
2. FEATURE_TRAIL_POOL_SIZE - pool size (2 per side)
3. FEATURE_TRAIL_ANIM_NAME - animation name "In"
4. FEATURE_TRAIL_DEFAULT_SKIN - default skin name "default" for fallback
5. FEATURE_TRAIL_LR_SPLIT - the split point (3) for determining left vs right trail based on roundIndex

These are configuration constants that will be used by the implementation logic. This is part of building out the feature.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Added FeatureWheel trail animation configuration constants</title>
  <subtitle>Six static constants defined for trail pool management, animation control, and left/right selection logic</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>FEATURE_TRAIL_POOL_KEY_L constant set to "FeatureTrailL" for left trail object pool</fact>
    <fact>FEATURE_TRAIL_POOL_KEY_R constant set to "FeatureTrailR" for right trail object pool</fact>
    <fact>FEATURE_TRAIL_POOL_SIZE constant set to 2 for pre-allocation per side</fact>
    <fact>FEATURE_TRAIL_ANIM_NAME constant set to "In" as the single animation key</fact>
    <fact>FEATURE_TRAIL_DEFAULT_SKIN constant set to "default" for invalid skinName fallback</fact>
    <fact>FEATURE_TRAIL_LR_SPLIT constant set to 3 as the roundIndex threshold for left/right selection</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Added configuration constants for the FeatureWheel trail animation system in FlyManager. Two separate pool keys (FeatureTrailL and FeatureTrailR) enable independent object pooling for left and right trail animations, each pre-allocated with 2 instances. The animation name "In" is defined as the single Spine animation to play. A default skin constant provides fallback behavior when invalid skin names are passed. The split point constant (3) encodes the core selection logic: roundIndex values less than 3 use the left trail, values 3 or greater use the right trail, implementing the 0-2 vs 3-5 round split. These constants centralize configuration values referenced throughout the implementation, following the existing pattern of static readonly constants for pool keys, sizes, and animation parameters.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>assets/Script/FlyManager.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

  <narrative>Added the PLAY_FEATURE_WHEEL_TRAIL_ANIMATION event to the EVENTS_TO_REGISTER array, integrating it into FlyManager's automatic event lifecycle management. When the component loads, the RegisterEvent method iterates through this array and registers all listed events with the event manager, binding them to this component instance. Similarly, UnregisterEvent cleans up these registrations when the component is destroyed. This ensures the new feature wheel trail animation event will be properly wired up to the OnEvent dispatcher without requiring manual registration calls, following the established pattern used by the other four animation events in the system.</narrative>

The pools will be used to retrieve and return trail nodes during gameplay.</narrative>

5. Node retrieval from pool with error handling
6. BaseSpine component retrieval with validation
7. Spine activation and world position setup
8. Trail_End_Control bone lookup with error handling

10. Animation name and duration retrieval
11. Animation playback

13. Await for animation duration divided by Game_Define.SpeedUp

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Implemented PlayFeatureWheelTrailAnimation core method with complete trail animation logic</title>
  <subtitle>98-line async method handles left/right trail selection, Spine animation, bone positioning, skin switching, and object pool lifecycle</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Method determines left vs right trail using roundIndex < FEATURE_TRAIL_LR_SPLIT comparison (threshold of 3)</fact>
    <fact>Retrieves trail node from appropriate pool (FeatureTrailL or FeatureTrailR) based on selection</fact>
    <fact>Validates prefab existence, node retrieval, and BaseSpine component availability with early returns</fact>
    <fact>Positions Spine node at startWorldPosition using setWorldPosition for trail origin</fact>
    <fact>Locates Trail_End_Control bone and positions it at endWorldPosition converted to spine-local coordinates</fact>
    <fact>Switches Spine skin with SetSkin, falling back to "default" for null/invalid skinName values</fact>
    <fact>Plays "In" animation and calculates duration adjusted by Game_Define.SpeedUp for timing synchronization</fact>
    <fact>Awaits animation completion, deactivates node, returns to pool, and invokes callback</fact>
    <fact>Try-catch block ensures cleanup and callback invocation even on errors</fact>
    <fact>Implementation added at lines 742-839 in FlyManager.ts before SetReelSymbolMode method</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Implemented the complete PlayFeatureWheelTrailAnimation method that orchestrates feature wheel trail visual effects. The method begins by using roundIndex to determine whether to use the left or right trail variant (rounds 0-2 use left, 3-5 use right), then selects the corresponding pool key and prefab reference. After validating the prefab is assigned, it retrieves an effect node from PrefabPoolManager and obtains its BaseSpine component. The Spine node is activated and positioned at the start world position to establish the trail's origin. The method locates the Trail_End_Control bone and converts the end world position to spine-local coordinates (using getNodeSpaceAR on the Spine's node to avoid parent scale issues) before setting the bone's x/y position. It switches to the requested skin (with "default" as fallback), retrieves the "In" animation's duration, and plays the animation. The method awaits the animation duration divided by Game_Define.SpeedUp to account for game speed multipliers, then deactivates the Spine, returns the node to the pool, and invokes the optional callback. A try-catch wrapper ensures that even on errors, the node is properly returned and the callback fires. This implementation follows the same defensive patterns as existing animation methods (PlayShowScatterToMagicPotAnimation, PlayFlyToCollectAnimation), using spine-node coordinate spaces, thorough validation, and guaranteed cleanup.</narrative>
